revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Atagoyama Kofun (Mito)]][[ja:愛宕山古墳 (水戸市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Atagoyama Kofun | native_name =愛宕山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Atagoyama Kofun (Mito).JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Atago JInja, where the Atagoyama Kofun is located | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Atagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 40 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3964|N|140.45165278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mito, Ibaraki]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 18,813 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Atagoyama Kofun'''|愛宕山古墳 ||qq=}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Atago neighborhood of the city of [[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/434 |title=愛宕山古墳 |trans-title= Atagoyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Atagoyama Kofun is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} on the west bank of the Nakagawa River at an elevation of 40 meters. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and is one of the best-preserved keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in eastern Japan. It is also one the largest found in Ibaraki Prefecture.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus has a total length of 136.5 meters with an orientation of northwest to southeast and was surrounded by a moat with a width of 23 meters. The tumulus is well-preserved, but the moat has mostly been filled in and is occupied by private residences. The ''kofun'' is named for a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, and [[Atago Gongen|Atago Shrine]] located at its summit.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The interior of the tomb has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found in the vicinity in 1978 survey. Large cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found on the ridges and skirts of the posterior part of the posterior dome, so it is estimated that there were originally 3 to 4 rows of ''haniwa''. From the style of the ''haniwa'' it is believed to have been built in the early 6th century as the tomb of Takekashima-no-mikoto, the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of the "Nakanokuni", whose territories existed on either side of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is one of a cluster of ''kofun'' in the vicinity, which were not accorded protected status. The tumulus is a five-minute walk from the "Hakamatsuka 2-chome” bus stop on the Ibaraki Kotsu Bus from {{ill|Mito Station (Ibaraki)|en|Mito Station (Ibaraki)|qid=Q909499|lt=Mito Station|1=Mito Station (Ibaraki)}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Total length: 136.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 73 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 78 meter diameter x 10 meters high ==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan|qq=}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|qid=Q16248240|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== * [https://www.ibarakiguide.jp/db-kanko/atago_mt.html Ibaraki Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/kuni/shiseki/12-7/12-7.html Ibaraki Prefectural Board of Education] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.mito.lg.jp/001373/001374/0/shiteibunkazai/siteibunkazai/atagoyamakofun.html Mito City official site] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
